entry.title: Re: Issue with concatenate. No blanks at the end. RESPECTING BLANKS not working fully.
entry.content:
I did a test. I confirm that it works as I said (in fact, what says the SAP documentation):

For TRANSFER, you have to use a variable of type STRING or the word LENGTH if you use a variable of type C. TRANSFER - ABAP Keyword Documentation (sap.com):

If the file was opened as a text file or a legacy text file, the trailing blank characters are deleted for all data objects, except for those of data type string.

When I said that "probably you have used a way to download the file, which removes the [trailing blanks]", I meant that after the use of TRANSFER, you have retrieved the file in such a way that you didn't keep the trailing blanks. Anyway, you did something wrong, but you didn't provide enough information to clarify what exactly was wrong.

entry.title: Re: How is the "Missing Hours" is being calculated in "Manage My Timehseet" appl
entry.content: Added HR tag, please remove other tags.

entry.title: Re: Alternative labeling for function location & replacin...
entry.content:
IMHO teh aspects of mass changing Company code and Plant are completely unrelated to the question relating to alternative labelling (AL) for functional locations (FL).

Please check corresponding doc and notes for AL.
When activating it newly created FLs will have an internal ID (TPLNR '?nnnnnn...nnn') and the label will be stored in a filed STRNO. For existing FLs the TPLNR will not change from the 'readable' representation - but corresponding label might change over time.
Thus no impact on other areas referencing the FLs.
For mass change options please check mass change funcitonalities in the list reports for FLs.

For mass changing FL labels you will most probably have to call some FL API (BAPI, Odata service) in order to determine the new label individually.

entry.title: Re: SAP Vulnerability Issue
entry.content:
Hello,

if you are using SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition then I would think that the responsibility for patching should be with SAP. Or are you installing SAP S/4HANA On-Premise?

Anyway I would suggest you get the security patch information directly from the source at https://support.sap.com/en/my-support/knowledge-base/security-notes-news.html.

Best Regards
Gregor

entry.title: Re: Automatic Subaccount Migration to another Region
entry.content: I don't think that there are any tools supporting a subaccount migration. I would suggest you take the opportunity and setup the new subaccount with the Terraform Provider for SAP BTP.

entry.content: I would start at https://learning.sap.com/products/sap-build

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI for Java 8.10 on the Horizon

entry.title: Re: How to add fields to FTR02 screen?
entry.content:
In Program RFWEKO02 you need to implement implicit enhancement to add your fields at the end of gt_item1.

Then go to routine ALV_ROUTINES and implement another enhancment to bring the data :

"Not recommended to select inside a loop, better to make it "for all entries" "

Last step to add the new field to the field catalog in the same routine 

That is all.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano ,

I hope you are well,

I'm Mahmoud Mostafa an SAP MM Functional consultant from Egypt. I've been working in SAP for 3 Years Now. I have Knowledge on SAP S4 HANA Sourcing and Procurement but not certified.

I identified as a person in the below categories:
-People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts.
-first-generation college graduate in my family.
-People looking to switch their careers mid-life.

I will be very grateful if I take a free certification attempt because I believe it would help me to learn more knowledge in SAP and get more opportunities in my career.

Thanks a lot for this grateful Opportunity!

best Regards,

entry.title: Using Smart Links in Book Page Templates
entry.content:
Hi Community,

We are using this smart link $#{project:.uid}:demo (see picture below) to connect our demo/practice modes in our book page templates. This is very helpful in reducing the work of our authors.

However, I tried to apply this smart link to shapes and images, but it seems not to be working. Is this link applicable only to text? Is there a specific smart link for shapes and images?



Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 Web client does not show print layout same as...
entry.content:
Hi SonTran,

The report was created by Crystal Report. I am wondering when I preview the report from the SAP client I saw a lot of Crystal report in the list but when I open the preview layout from WebClient, it shows only 2 reports in the list. 

I try to import the new Crystal report from SAP client, still cannot see that report on Web Client.

I asked the ChatGPT and it recommended for option Available for Web Client that I cannot see this option even I try to use on SAP 10 2402. 

Any idea for this?

entry.title: Issue with Duplicate Invoice Check in Vendor Invoice Process
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are encountering an issue where the system is not performing a duplicate check for invoices posted through VIM.

Here’s the scenario we tested: A non-PO invoice was posted via VIM with the reference number "TEST1." However, when a user accidentally attempts to manually reprocess the same invoice through FB60, the system does not trigger an error or stop the process for duplicate invoice detection.

Please note, all duplicate invoice check parameters—such as company code, vendor, invoice date, reference number, currency, and amount—are identical in this case.

Looking forward to your assistance.

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to prevent ObjectPageSubSection from promoting to ObjectPageSection?
entry.content:
Have you tried adding a dummy section with empty title and no content? 
 
If i remeber properly, I did this previously.

entry.title: Re: "Operations in SAP HANA/BWA" in tab "Runtime Properti...
entry.content: we didn't change any objects for the report, I only change "Operations in SAP HANA/BWA" in tab "Runtime Properties" of the BW query as described above. I am not looking how to improve the performance. I am just curious why different operation setting caused different report result.

entry.title: Re: FM: ALM_ME_FUNCLOC_INSTALLED_EQUI
entry.content: Hi, The FM: ALM_ME_FUNCLOC_INSTALLED_EQUI accepts only single functional location at a time. you can search for a different function module or any released API for this purpose. Or you can create an internal table using the table type of this data element 'TPLNR' and call the FM inside the loop. Note: Try using FM 'ALM_ME_ML_FLOC_INST_EQUI' fill in the table 'IT_FUNCLOC_INST_EQUIS' if it works for your case.

entry.title: Re: "Operations in SAP HANA/BWA" in tab "Runtime Properti...
entry.content: there is not any objects' change for the report , including data source (sap hana view), BW objects (open ODS views etc), BOBJ objects (OLAP connection, report itself etc)

very informative article!

Br.

@Raja

entry.title: SAP CAP: req.error inside bound action not highlighting target in Fiori Elements Object Page
entry.content:
Hi guys,

I have a bound action in my sap cap project where I check for mandatory fields and throw errors highlighting the targets in the UI, in a Fiori Elements Object page. 

        if (mandatoryFields.length) {
            for (const field of mandatoryFields) {
                if (!item[field.mandatoryField]) {
                    req.error({
                            message: `The field ${field.description} is mandatory`,
                            target: `in/${field.mandatoryField}`,
                            code: 400,
                        });
                    }
            };
        };

 My problem here is that it is showing the error in the UI in a MessageBox but not highlighting the targets. I have the exact same code inside a this.on("SAVE") action and it works there, but somehow it doesn't inside a bound action.

The only solution I found is returning the req.error, but then it only shows it one by one and feels very clumsy.

I would appreciate any help with this issue. Thanks!

entry.title: Issue missing home page for a specific persona in the SAP2405 + missing some notes from 2405
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
@dudejas , @syam_yalamati 

Currently, we are accessing the SAP Service Asset Manager 2405 application within the S/4HANA(Version - 2022) and kyma environment. We can access the application through our credential but After the initial sync was successfully completed, we are encountering a missing home screen issue. We are unable to view the Home Page.

However We tried implementing notes(3398187) into SSAM2405, but it is not relevant for our system and states that it cannot be implemented. This could be due to the status of our S4HANA 2022 system, as it has S4FND SP level 0000.

I have attached the log screenshots for your reference

Could please provide some insights or suggestions to resolve this issue?





Thank you very much in advance,
Kingston. A

SAP HANA service for SAP BTP  , SAP Field Service Management, connector for SAP Business One  
#SAP Service and Asset Manager,   #SAP Mobile Services,  #SAP Mobile Asset Management , SAP BTP, Kyma runtime  , SAP HANA Enterprise Cloud  

@syam_yalamati , @dudejas

entry.title: Inbound Idoc is also getting generated with outbound Idoc
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I have done the configuration for Outbound Idoc for message type PROJECT and basic type PROJECT02. The outbound Idoc is getting triggerred correctly with status 03. But along with it inbound Idoc also getting triggerred with status 51. Any one knows why the inbound Idoc is getting triggerred.

Thanks,

Vidhya

entry.title: Re: SAP Inside Track Munich 2024 (#sitMUC) - Secure Your Spot Now!
entry.content:
If you go to Garching Research Center (Garching-Forschungszentrum) by subway or buses, you have to walk through the building of the mechanical engineering faculty, You'll see a sign to walk to SAP Labs. Following Google Maps is absolutely not recommended.

From the airport: The best alternative is to go by S1 to Neufahrn, and from there take bus 690/691 towards Garching-Hochbrück to the "Forschungsreaktor" stop. A second option is to go by S8 to Ismaning, and from there take bus 230 towards Garching TU.

entry.title: Re: 🇩🇪 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core

If you go to Garching Research Center (Garching-Forschungszentrum) by subway or buses, you have to walk through the building of the mechanical engineering faculty, You'll see a sign to walk to SAP Labs. Following Google Maps is absolutely not recommended.

From the airport: The best alternative is to go by S1 to Neufahrn, and from there take bus 690/691 towards Garching-Hochbrück to the "Forschungsreaktor" stop. A second option is to go by S8 to Ismaning, and from there take bus 230 towards Garching TU.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to integrate a sapui5 BarcodeScanner i...
entry.content:
Hi,

CAP is a Backend technology providing APIs in the form of OData, REST and GraphQL. Maybe your misunderstanding comes from to the possibility to annotate the OData Service with UI annotations. That allow you to quickly get also SAP Fiori Element UIs. But of couse you can create also your custom UI5 apps that make use of the sap.ndc.sample.BarcodeScannerButton.

Best Regards
Gregor

Kr

Ben

entry.title: Vendor Return process
entry.content:
What is return process - Return to supplier processing enables you to return damaged or unwanted products from your supplier.

Generally we are creating new return po to return the material.

Vendor return process with same PO this helps to return the stock with same price and also you can track the return in PO History.

Process 

  •  Create purchase order by using ME21N t code.
  • Create inbound delivery by using VL31N - By logistic department.

What is  inbound delivery - INBOUND DELIVERY in SAP pertains to all incoming goods, which may refer to either a vendor delivery or a return of a rejected delivery to a customer. It may cover the schedules of deliveries from your vendors/suppliers or even a rejected delivery to a customer. 

  • Create Transfer order - By using LT03 t code and confirm the transfer order by using LT12 t code 

The Transfer order or requirement contains information about a planned movement of stock in the warehouse. 

  • Post goods receipt - By using  MIGO t code 
  • Post Invoice receipt - By using MIRO t code procurement

Vendor Return Process with same PO 

  • Create Return Delivery against above posted GR document - Check the flag via Delivery in MIGO Use Movement type 122/124
  • Display the Outbound delivery which was created - VL03N t code.

When you send any material out from the manufacturing plant it is called outbound delivery. While selling, you dispatch the material which is called outbound delivery.

  • Create transfer order and confirm the same - By using LT03 t code and confirm the transfer order by using LT12 t code 
  • Post Goods issue - By using VL02N 
  • Post credit Memo - By using MIRO t code

Upon receiving the returned goods, the vendor issues a credit memo. This document serves as a confirmation of the return and details the amount to be credited to the organization.

Differences between Vendor Return with same PO and Different return PO:

 

Vendor Return with same PO 

Vendor Return with new return PO

  • Return of the goods cab ne track in same PO history
  • Unable to link return po with original purchase order
  • Goods can be return with same price
  • Goods can be return with same or different price
  • Returns movement type are 122/124
  • Returns movement type are 161

 A well-documented return process supports compliance with regulatory requirements and internal controls

entry.title: System access

entry.title: SAP Learning path for a candidate with technical development background
entry.content:
Greetings Everyone!!! I am exploring a career around SAP technologies. I do have development backgound of middleware and database technologies using C# and JAVA, cloud, perfromance engineering, management with close to over all 18+ years of experience. I would like to get some suggestions from SAP community and also if anyone made a move switching to SAp from traditional application/services developement.

I am looking for infights on what't the best course to make a switch and what do you suggest to start with, focus  more and get hands-on experience or get trained on and suggested certifications on SAP modules. I would like to focus more on technology rathar than functional.

Appreciate your insights and more importantly your time.

Have a great weekend

Mohan

entry.title: Strengthening High Availability for SAP in AWS Environments
entry.content:
SAP has over 437,000 customers in 190 countries. It is a leading provider of enterprise applications and supply chain management. SAP systems are integral to business operations and demand a highly reliable infrastructure to ensure uninterrupted service. Implementing high availability (HA) in AWS requires well thought out planning, especially when integrating tools like Pacemaker to manage SAP services and databases. This guide will delve into advanced strategies to enhance the robustness of HA setups, ensuring that SAP systems remain operational and resilient against disruptions.

In this blog with coauthor Santa Uppala (Architect), we will focus on Suse implementation,  most of which can be easily adjusted to Redhat. We will share the lessons learned through years of real customer experiences, combined with the latest AWS and SAP guidelines. 

We start with a way to simplify resources in HA setup called "Simple-Mount". Then we continue on to other important topics.

Simple-Mount Configuration

SUSE has introduced a new ASCS and ERS cluster solution using a simple-mount file system structure. This simplifies the NFS shared file system and relies on SYSTEMD for managing filesystem dependencies, enhancing robustness over traditional configurations. This is now the recommended default for both NetWeaver ENSA1 and S/4HANA ENSA2 (Amazon Web Services) (SUSE Documentation).

Traditionally, we control EFS, for example, /usr/sap/SID/ASCS00 and /usr/sap/SID/ERS10, via cluster, and mount the relevant file system in one node of the cluster where the service is active. We have found that EFS could become a bottleneck and even cause instability issue when doing switching over. We have been trying to find a simplified approach.  Suse recently proposed Simple Mount Architecture where we no longer need to control EFS resources by cluster. Instead, we simply mount EFS on all cluster nodes.

Here is a description of how to manage EFS.

  1. Maintain /etc/fstab on all nodes of the cluster. As a viable alternative, we could use systemd resource control for the same purpose and enable the resource so filesystems are mounted post reboot. 
  2. Mount on all nodes. Make sure to check if all filesystems are automatically mounted after reboot.
  3. In cluster resource groups, do not configure resources to manage EFS. Remove such configurations if already existing.

For more information, please refer to

Simple Mount Structure for SAP Application Platform | SUSE Communities

 

Cluster Failover Monitoring

It is critical to monitor HA and got alerted when it happens so basis could check SAP systems to ensure all SAP systems are operational and all batch jobs are properly handled. Here we show a concrete case of failover monitoring and Service*Now incident generation.

When cluster failover happens, cluster manager will perform a series of actions to trigger the main application service failover from one node to the other node in the cluster. From AWS perspective, there is a ReplaceRoute event, which we could capture and then setup necessary actions to alert administrators.

From AWS console, we create a cloudwatch rule which will be triggered by ReplaceRoute event. And this event will designate a SNS target that can be subscribed by email thread and Service*Now incident trigger.

Here is the setup,

Event pattern

{
  "source": ["aws.ec2"],
  "detail-type": ["AWS API Call via CloudTrail"],
  "detail": {
    "eventSource": ["ec2.amazonaws.com"],
    "eventName": ["ReplaceRoute"]
  }
}

In this event rule, we setup its target to a SNS topic, which Service*Now could subscribe and trigger incidents and alerts.

 

Monitor SAP Resources using Cloudwatch Application Insights

Using Application Insights we can monitor SAP Resources such as Central services and HANA DB operational status. Dashboards can be created as a single pane of glass to monitor the performance and availability at database, resources, OS and AWS Infrastructure layers. Additionally, it correlates and analyzes log data to identify warnings of common problems such as sync state,  resource utilization, replication issues and critical process failures in the HANA database.

Redundant Cluster Communication:

It is recommended to define a redundant communication channel (a second ring) in Corosync for SUSE clusters. This improves fault tolerance by providing a backup communication path between cluster nodes (Amazon AWS Documentation).

e.g.

nodelist {
        node {
                ring0_addr: 11.0.1.132
                ring1_addr: 11.0.1.75
               nodeid: 1
        }

        node {
                ring0_addr: 11.0.2.139
                ring1_addr: 11.0.2.35
                nodeid: 2
        }
}

We can refer to

https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sap/latest/sap-hana/sap-hana-on-aws-cluster-configuration.html

The conversion from single ring to dual ring can be done without any downtime. Here are the steps:

  1. Get another set of IPs by adding IP from AWS console
    EC2 -> Networking -> Manage IP addresses -> Assign more IPs.
  2. Change corosync by using the following command on all nodes
    crm corosync edit
  3. Enable the new configuration
    corosync-cfgtool -R
  4. Validate the configuration
    corosync-cmapctl

If outage is allowed, we can do a “crm cluster stop” and make changes to /etc/corosync/corosync.conf, 
then do “crm cluster start”

Monitoring Location Constraints using Custom Scripts

One of the most important aspects in the HA Resource failover is the location constraints. Sometimes, back and forth failover creates a negative location constraint in the CRM config file which acts as a lock mechanism instructing the DC node not to failover the resources at any given circumstances, this will result in the unsuccessful failover if undetected. In order to avoid this we need to setup a custom monitoring to detect these location lock entries and delete it in timely manner. 

Cluster Resource Configuration via AWS Launch Wizard

AWS Launch Wizard has become increasing popular and helped cloud architect/engineers to automate the provisioning the SAP clusters with pacemaker. Here is a reference on the topic.
Simplify, Accelerate, and Automate SAP Deployment Using AWS Launch Wizard for SAP

We illustrate the results from using AWS Launch Wizard for SAP, where all the best practices and latest recommendations are already built in. We will illustrate with ASCS/ERS instances as it embodies the recommendations such as simple mount. 

Here is the crm status,

Node List:
* Online: [ nodeps01 nodeps02 ]

Full List of Resources:
* res_AWS_STONITH (stonith:external/ec2): Started nodeps02
* Resource Group: grp_DWS_ASCS01:
* rsc_ip_DWS_ASCS01 (ocf::suse:aws-vpc-move-ip): Started nodeps02
* rsc_sap_DWS_ASCS01 (ocf::heartbeat:SAPInstance): Started nodeps02
* Resource Group: grp_DWS_ERS10:
* rsc_ip_DWS_ERS10 (ocf::suse:aws-vpc-move-ip): Started nodeps01
* rsc_sap_DWS_ERS10 (ocf::heartbeat:SAPInstance): Started nodeps01


Now the whole configuration as a reference,

node 1: nodeps01 \
attributes maintenance=off
node 2: nodeps02 \
attributes maintenance=off
primitive res_AWS_STONITH stonith:external/ec2 \
op start interval=0 timeout=180 \
op stop interval=0 timeout=180 \
op monitor interval=120 timeout=60 \
params tag=dwspacemaker profile=cluster pcmk_delay_max=15
primitive rsc_ip_DWS_ASCS01 ocf:suse:aws-vpc-move-ip \
params ip=192.168.10.20 routing_table=rtb-12345678 interface=eth0 profile=cluster \
op start interval=0 timeout=180 \
op stop interval=0 timeout=180 \
op monitor interval=60 timeout=60 \
meta target-role=Started
primitive rsc_ip_DWS_ERS10 ocf:suse:aws-vpc-move-ip \
params ip=192.168.10.30 routing_table=rtb-12345678 interface=eth0 profile=cluster \
op start interval=0 timeout=180 \
op stop interval=0 timeout=180 \
op monitor interval=60 timeout=60 \
meta target-role=Started
primitive rsc_sap_DWS_ASCS01 SAPInstance \
operations $id=rsc_sap_DWS_ASCS01-operations \
op monitor interval=120 timeout=60 on-fail=restart \
params InstanceName=DWS_ASCS01_nodepa01 START_PROFILE="/sapmnt/DWS/profile/DWS_ASCS01_nodepa01" AUTOMATIC_RECOVER=false \
meta resource-stickiness=5000 failure-timeout=60 migration-threshold=1 priority=10 target-role=Started
primitive rsc_sap_DWS_ERS10 SAPInstance \
operations $id=rsc_sap_DWS_ERS10-operations \
op monitor interval=120 timeout=60 on-fail=restart \
params InstanceName=DWS_ERS10_nodeer01 START_PROFILE="/sapmnt/DWS/profile/DWS_ERS10_nodeer01" AUTOMATIC_RECOVER=false IS_ERS=true \
meta priority=1000 target-role=Started
group grp_DWS_ASCS01 rsc_ip_DWS_ASCS01 rsc_sap_DWS_ASCS01 \
meta resource-stickiness=3000 target-role=Started
group grp_DWS_ERS10 rsc_ip_DWS_ERS10 rsc_sap_DWS_ERS10
colocation col_sap_DWS_no_both -5000: grp_DWS_ERS10 grp_DWS_ASCS01
location loc_sap_DWS_failover_to_ers rsc_sap_DWS_ASCS01 \
rule 2000: runs_ers_DWS eq 1
order ord_sap_DWS_first_start_ascs Optional: rsc_sap_DWS_ASCS01:start rsc_sap_DWS_ERS10:stop symmetrical=false
property cib-bootstrap-options: \
have-watchdog=false \
dc-version="2.1.5+20221208.a3f44794f-150500.6.14.4-2.1.5+20221208.a3f44794f" \
cluster-infrastructure=corosync \
maintenance-mode=false \
stonith-enabled=true \
stonith-action=off \
stonith-timeout=600s \
last-lrm-refresh=1716118611
rsc_defaults rsc-options: \
resource-stickiness=1 \
migration-threshold=3
op_defaults op-options: \
timeout=600 \
record-pending=trueINFO: "config" is accepted as "configure"

Conclusion:

Maintaining healthy cluster configuration is paramount to the stability of SAP systems. We have observed positive results after addressing the points outlined in this blog. It is an iterative process, and we will continue this innovative journey to minimize system interruptions, ensure proper alerts, and enhance our ability to handle exceptions.

References:

Simple Mount Structure for SAP Application Platform

First certified simple-mount cluster ever!

SAP S/4 HANA - Enqueue Replication 2 High Availability Cluster With Simple Mount

Updated Guidance for SAP Pacemaker configuration – ABAP SAP Central Services (ASCS)

SAP NetWeaver on AWS High Availability for SLES Planning

entry.title: How can we apply filters on navigation properties of associated entities in a SAP CAP OData service
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have three entities: Entity A, Entity B, and Entity C in our SAP CAP model. There are associations between Entity A and both Entity B and Entity C.

We want to write a select query to retrieve data from Entity A by applying filters on the properties of Entity B and Entity C. However, we are facing difficulty in constructing the correct query to apply filters on the navigation properties at the service level. Could someone please provide guidance on how to write this query?

FYI:- We are using NodeJS

Thanks in advance.
SAP Cloud Application Programming Model  SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment #Nodejs

entry.title: Re: Troubleshooting SAP Certification discount code for special programs
entry.content:
I successfully redeemed, but it shows it take sometime to process your booking. What I want to do now ?

May I wait for sometime or What I want to do ? I need clarification from SAP Community.

entry.title: Re: Issue while loading data from CSV to HDBTABLE
entry.content: Were you able to find a solution for this issue?

entry.title: SAP CAP Error during OData GET request "504 Gateway Timeout" after 30 sec.
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am getting this 504 Gateway Timeout issue after 30 sec when sending a OData get request in my SAP CAP NodeJS Application.

How to make an OData GET request having huge data?
How can i increase the timeout beyond seconds to minutes?

FYI:-
1. I am using postgres database in my CAP Application.
2.i chunked down that OData request to smaller Odata request but still facing the same issue.
2. I have added parameter 'pool' to 'requires'->'db' configuration also but that didnt work.

Thanks in advance.

SAP Cloud Application Programming Model PostgreSQL on SAP BTP, hyperscaler option #SAP BTP

entry.title: SAP BUILD APPS is showing subscription error even with booster
entry.content:
Hi All, even after trying several ways BTP trial account is not able to subscribe SAP Build Apps, Can anyone help here.

Thanks in Advance.

entry.title: Re: 🟢 It's Time for TypeScript (CAP edition)

entry.title: Devtoberfest sessions timezone - Accomodating EST
entry.content:
HI Team - Excited to join the group and feeling regrets that I missed till now.

I was going through the events and see most of then start in BST time zone which will be too early for EST.

So will the sessions being recorded and be available to play on demand or is it mandatory to join at the specified time?

If recorded and available, where can we access the same? 

Regards,

Madhu

entry.title: Transformation Office Roadmap: Your Essential Navigation Tool for Successful Business Transformation
entry.content:
What is a Transformation Office Roadmap?

The Transformation Office Roadmap is a hands-on guide that breaks down your transformation process into clear, actionable steps. It gives your team a shared vision, keeps them aligned, and helps track progress at every stage. The roadmap covers key areas such as:

  • Defining your transformation’s vision and mission
  • Clarifying roles and responsibilities within the Transformation Office
  • Mapping out transformation phases and key milestones
  • Building a communication strategy to keep everyone informed
  • Managing resources and budget allocation
  • Developing risk management and change management processes
  • Setting KPIs to measure success

Why Do You Need a Transformation Office Roadmap?

Think of your transformation like a long journey. Without a roadmap, you're likely to encounter unnecessary detours, confusion, and missed opportunities. Over the years, I've seen organizations struggle when they don’t have a clear path, and this often leads to delays, misalignment, and ultimately, failure to reach the desired outcomes.

The Transformation Office Roadmap provides that critical structure, ensuring that everyone knows their role, the timeline, and the expected outcomes. It brings clarity, accountability, and a sense of shared purpose.

The Roadmap Exercise: How It Works

This interactive exercise will guide you through each component of the roadmap, allowing you to actively apply the concepts to your own business transformation. Here’s how we’ll build it step by step:

Step 1: Define the Vision & Mission of Your Transformation

  • Goal: Establish the “why” behind your transformation, giving everyone a clear sense of purpose.

  • Exercise: Have each participant define their business’s main objective for transformation and summarize the mission of their Transformation Office.

    Example section:

    • Business Goal: ___________________________________________
    • Transformation Mission: ___________________________________

Step 2: Establish Key Roles and Responsibilities

  • Goal: Ensure everyone understands their part in the transformation.

  • Exercise: Identify and document the core roles within the Transformation Office, including who’s responsible for what.

    Example section:

    • Transformation Office Lead: _______________________________
    • Change Manager: ________________________________________
    • Stakeholder Manager: ____________________________________

Step 3: Map the Phases of Transformation

  • Goal: Break the transformation down into manageable phases.

  • Exercise: List out the major phases and identify the key milestones within each one.

    Example section:

    • Phase 1 – Strategy & Planning: _____________________________
    • Phase 2 – Execution & Rollout: _____________________________
    • Key Milestones: __________________________________________

Step 4: Create a Communication Strategy

  • Goal: Ensure that your stakeholders remain engaged and informed.

  • Exercise: Plan out the communication channels and how often updates will be shared with different stakeholders.

    Example section:

    • Communication Channels: ________________________________
    • Frequency (Weekly, Monthly, etc.): _________________________
    • Target Audience (C-Level, Employees, etc.): _________________

Step 5: Identify Resources & Budget

  • Goal: Allocate the necessary resources and budget for each phase of the transformation.

  • Exercise: Document the required resources and estimate the overall budget.

    Example section:

    • Personnel Requirements: __________________________________
    • Technology/Tools Needed: _________________________________
    • Estimated Budget: ________________________________________

Step 6: Develop a Risk and Change Management Plan

  • Goal: Prepare for potential roadblocks and set up proactive change management processes.

  • Exercise: Brainstorm risks for each phase and develop mitigation strategies.

    Example section:

    • Risk 1: _______________________________________________
    • Risk 2: _______________________________________________
    • Mitigation Strategy: ____________________________________

Step 7: Define KPIs for Success Measurement

  • Goal: Establish metrics to track progress and measure success.

  • Exercise: Select 3-5 KPIs that will act as performance indicators for your transformation.

    Example section:

    • KPI 1: _________________________________________________
    • KPI 2: _________________________________________________
    • KPI 3: _________________________________________________

How to Conduct the Exercise:

Before the session:
  • Share the Transformation Office Roadmap template with participants (either digitally or as a physical handout).

During the session:

  • Guide participants through each section, allowing time for reflection and group discussion after every major point. Encourage participants to fill out each section based on their own organizational context.
After the session:
  • Wrap up with a reflection session, where participants can share their completed roadmaps. This peer-sharing helps everyone refine their approach and gain new insights.
  • Encourage them to take their roadmap back to their teams and continue using it as a working document.

Why This Roadmap Works

The Transformation Office Roadmap offers much more than just a list of tasks — it is a dynamic and interactive tool that engages participants throughout the transformation process. By creating the roadmap in real-time, each person takes ownership of the process and actively applies what they’ve learned to their specific challenges.

This exercise fosters accountability, collaboration, and ensures that the transformation stays aligned with business goals. Moreover, participants will leave with a tangible plan that serves as a living document, guiding them throughout the transformation.

Take Charge of Your Transformation Journey

Transforming your business is a complex and ongoing process, but with the right roadmap, you can navigate the challenges smoothly and confidently. The Transformation Office Roadmap provides your team with the focus and clarity needed to turn a grand vision into actionable steps.

So, let me share one final tip from experience: With a roadmap in hand, you’re not just driving transformation — you’re steering it towards success.

Ready to start your journey?

Gather your team and begin crafting your Transformation Office Roadmap today!

And one final question to all of you: Anyone experienced something similar or has another great tool to share? Let`s discuss ...

entry.title: Re: BW/4HANA Cockpit - process-chain-monitor no longer sh...
entry.content:
The ODQ_TQ_JOB is managed by the ODP (Operational Data Provisioning) framework. It should be scheduled automatically by the system under normal circumstances.

Run ODQMON and go to Settings > ODP Framework Jobs.
See if the ODQ_TQ_JOB is listed there. If it's missing, you can try to reactivate it by going to ODQ Settings.

OR Schedule the job manually in sm36 if the ODP framework fails to do so. use Program: ODQ_TQ_JOB_START

Use transaction SM37 to review the background job logs to confirm whether ODQ_TQ_JOB is being scheduled and running successfully.

entry.title: Re: "Operations in SAP HANA/BWA" in tab "Runtime Properti...
entry.content:
Either your Query has some complex CKF or RKF which are behaving differentlly or the HANA calc view logic is complex.

Check if the record count is getting truncated in runtime. There is a parameter which controls the count when the analyzer run complex execution plan. I am taking about backend record count not frontend excel record count.

In RSRT, execute the query with different settings for "Operations in HANA/BWA" and compare the results.
Option 1: Run with no optimized operations (everything in BW).
Option 2: Run with optimized operations in HANA and see where the result starts to deviate.
Use the debugging mode to trace the exact points where calculations or operations differ between BW and HANA.

entry.title: Re: How to Understand the HANA Generated CalculationScenario
entry.content:
The script you're referring to in the generated object (e.g., "<Schema-Name>"."0BW:BIA:CP1") is a Calculation Scenario. Calculation Scenario is the underlying runtime model that SAP HANA uses to represent complex calculations and data flows. When you create a Calculation View in SAP HANA (e.g., CV1 or CV2), what you design graphically in the SAP HANA Modeler or Web IDE is transformed into a Calculation Scenario.These Calculation Scenarios consist of nodes (e.g., aggregation, join, projection) and parameters that define the data flow and computation.

If you need to analyze the execution or logic of a Calculation View, SAP HANA provides tools like PlanViz, which allows you to see how the view is processed at runtime. This visualization helps you understand performance, the data flow, and where calculations are pushed down.

Regarding your 2nd question:

yes, In SAP HANA, input parameters are an integral part of the Calculation View design, and their values are passed along the chain of views and nodes during query execution.

Generated Calculation View (system-local.bw.bw2hana/CP1): This view corresponds to the HCPR (CP1) and includes input parameters that are linked to the underlying HANA CVs (e.g., CV1, CV2).
Input Parameter Propagation. When this generated view is executed (either directly or indirectly via another CV), the input parameters are passed down the hierarchy.
Input parameters from the higher-level Calculation View (CP1) will be propagated to its underlying nodes, which in your case are the base CVs (CV1, CV2).
If the underlying views (CV1, CV2) are designed with input parameters, these parameters will receive the values provided at the higher level (in CP1), enabling parameter-driven filtering, aggregation, or calculations.

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP Error during OData GET request "504 Gateway Timeout" after 30 sec.
entry.content: Can you provide some insight how huge your data is?

entry.title: Message Class in BTP ABAP environment no display authorization
entry.content:
===========================================================

Diagnosis

You attempted to displaya Workbench object but have no display authorization.
You attempted to change a Workbench object, but have neither the change nor the display authorization.

System Response

Object display refused.

Procedure

The system uses the authorization object S_DEVELOP for Workbench objects, butyour user master record contains no authorization for S_DEVELOP.
If necessary, ask your system administrator to give you this authorization.
==================================================================
Hey experts,

I can not activate a message class in my BTP ABAP environment trial account. I'm getting above error message in Eclipse. 

I've seen a YouTube video that developer add a message class (26:28 mins) in trial account without any errors.

How can I add S_DEVELOP authorization object? Or what should I do?

Vielen Dank im Voraus.

entry.title: Re: HCM Time Transfer Creation
entry.content: It is hard to debug time configuration without more information, such as Quota Groupings for these 2 employees, Quota Validity Periods, etc. and more information on the PCRs that create those 3 Time Type (Z261, Z262, Z263) and the Function that call the PCRs and it's placement in the Schema. For PERNR 00112, Z262 disapears in 202212, while for PERNR 11111, Z262 disapears in 202211. Did anything happen to these employees in that month (WPBP change, LOA, etc.) ?

entry.title: Re: Send alert when time account balance exceeds a specified number of days
entry.content: The organisation I used to work for had created an ABAP program that would read "Sick Leave" Quota Balances and generate an eMail to the employee if that Balance was less than x number of days. The program could be executed "manually" or "on schedule".

entry.title: Material Master Nomenclature
entry.content:
We have a  requirement where in the legacy system the material code is for 12 digits, the breakup of the Material code is first 2 digits represents the Main Group of the material, the next 2 Digits represents the Sub Group of the Material, the next 2 Digits represents the Sub-Sub Group of the Material and the next 2 Digits represents the category code of the material and the last four digits are the running numbers.

For Example - 

Main Group - 91 (IT materials)

Sub Group - 23 ( Hardware)

Sub Sub Group - 41 (Monitors)

Category code - 51 ( 17 inch)

Hence Material code is - 91234151 0001 (17 inch Dell Monitor)

How do we map this material’s nomenclature into SAP’s Material Master without product hierarchy? User wants to see a list of materials based upon either of Main Group, Sub group, Sub Sub group, Category Code.

Regards,

Sagnik

entry.title: Exploring Accrual Engine By Examples Part 1 - Manual Accrual
entry.content:
1. Introduction

In SAP S/4HANA 1809, the Accrual Engine was introduced to simplify and automate accrual accounting. This feature automates the calculation and posting of accruals, allowing businesses to manage finances more efficiently. The Accrual Engine integrates with the SAP General Ledger, providing improved accuracy and visibility in financial statements.

In this post, I’ll walk you through the key features of the Accrual Engine, its benefits, configuration examples, and how it helps streamline financial processes with practical use cases.

2. Accrual Defination

An accrual is an accounting method where revenues and expenses are recorded when they are earned or incurred, regardless of when the cash transaction takes place. This method reflects economic events when they occur, ensuring that financial statements show an accurate picture of a company's financial health by aligning revenues and expenses with the correct periods.

Example:

Jason Trading Company rents an office for 2,000 Euro per month. They start use the office in August but pay the rent in September.

In August, the company records an accrued liability of 2,000 Euro, showing that the rent expense has been incurred even though the payment hasn't been made. In September, once the rent is paid, the liability is cleared, and the cash outflow is recorded.

Journal Entries:

  • August – Accrual of Rent Expense (2,000 Euro):

    • Debit: Rent Expense GL (P&L) – €2,000
    • Credit: Accrued Rent Liability GL (Balance Sheet) – €2,000

  • September – Payment of Rent:

    • Debit: Accrued Rent Liability GL (Balance Sheet) – €2,000
    • Credit: Bank/Cash GL (Balance Sheet) – €2,000

This process ensures that the rent expense is recorded in the month the office was used (August), even though the payment was made later in September.

3. Accrual Engine

3.1 Key Benefits

Before the introduction of the new accrual engine, businesses often had to rely on manual processes to handle accruals, and this may cause inconsistencies and errors. The new engine addresses many of these issues by:

  • Posting accruals to the Universal Journal, supporting multiple currencies and ledger types.
  • Efficient reversal handling via standard SAP transactions such as FB08 (for single reversals) and F.80 (for mass reversals).
  • Streamlining the accrual process with review and approval steps, reducing time spent on month-end closing.
  • Allowing for custom posting rules for more complex scenarios.
  • Carrying forward accrual-related balances through Transaction FAGLGVT.

There are three types of accrual funcitons delivered with the Accrual Engine:

  • Manual Accrual (ACAC)
  • Purchase Order Accrual (POAC)
  • Service Entry Sheets Accruals (SSAC)

In this blog series, I will maninly introduce Manual Accrual and Purchase Order Accrual (next blog).

3.2 Manual Accrual

Let’s use a real example to explain how Manual Accrual works.

Jason Trading Company rents an office for 2,000 Euro per month and signs a 3-month contract starting in August.

a. Create an Accrual Object by transaction code ACACTREE01 (ACAC -> Manual Accrual)

  • Company Code: ZJC1 (Jason Trading Company)
  • Accrual Object Category: RENTING
  • Start of Life: 01.08.2024, End of Life: 31.10.2024
  • Accrual Item Type: COSTS
  • Ledger Group: 0L (posting to the Universal Journal and multiple ledgers)
  • Total Amount: 6,000 Euro
  • Accrual Method: LINEAR (spreads the accrual amounts evenly over the period)

     

      Once saved, an accrual object is created, and a financial document is automatically posted.

     

      The financial document gets posted because the Accrual Transaction Type 'Opening Positng'  (IP) is configured for the Accrual Item Type (COSTS) and the Company Code (ZJC1) in the configuration point 'Assign Accrual Item Types to Journal Entry Types and Posting Schemas' under transaction code ACEIMG.

 

      Now, let's take a look on the financial document.                    

       

89000 (Accrual Account, balancesheet) 6,000 EUR (-)A credit entry increases the accrual liability, recording the future obligation to pay rent.
211000 (Accrual Offset Account, cost -> profit & lose)6,000 EUR (+) A debit entry increases the rent expense in the P&L, showing that the cost has been incurred.

The accounts are read from configuration point 'Create and Edit Account Determination' under transaction code ACEIMG.

b. Posting Manual Accruals (ACEPOSTINGRUN)

To post accruals at the end of each month, we can run Transaction ACEPOSTINGRUN. Here’s how the postings unfold:

August:

The last day should be 31/08/2024.

After the posting (remove the Test Run flag), a FI document is posted.

Let's take a look on the document.

89000 (Accrual Account, balancesheet)2,000 EUR (+) Recognizing the rent expense for August in the P&L statement.
211000 (Accrual Offset Account, cost -> profit & lose) 2,000 EUR (-)Recording the accrued liability for August on the balance sheet.

Now, after refreshing the manual accrual object, we'll see that there are two postings.

The key different between the two posting is the 'Accrual Transaction Type'.

First posting: IP -> Open Posting 

Second posting: PP -> Period Posting (this posting happened each period)

September:

After the posting, a new document is posted,

From the screenshot, we can see that there are two line items in the list, both with the same document number.

Let's take a look on the document.

First and second line items: Accruals for August and September.

Third and fourth line items: Reversals (in this example, no manual reversal occurred before the end of September, and 'Post Deltas' was not selected for the Accrual Type 'Period Posting').

October:

After the posting, a new document is posted.

Similar to the document posted in September, there are four line items in this document.

First and second line items: Accruals for the three months.

Third and fourth line items: Reversals.

After the posting, refresh the manual accrual object and check the postings. From the list, we can see how the accrual was planned and processed over the three periods/months.

4. Conclusion

This blog is the first part of a series focused on exploring the Accrual Engine in SAP S/4HANA. In the next blog, we will dive into Purchase Order Accrual (POAC), explaining how it helps automate accruals for purchasing scenarios.

Follow me on LinkedIn to stay updated on more financial and technical posts.

https://www.linkedin.com/in/zhengzhang-jason-lu-42574b41/

entry.title: Re: Need to restrict the user's authorization to enable e...
entry.content:
Ask your BAsis/security team to control the access in PFCG role using below

Authorization Object: S_RS_TOOLS
Authorization Field: RS_TOOL
Value: RSRQ (Query Designer - Release Query for External Access)

Leave the RSRQ value empty for the users you don't want to give external excess to the query.

For more complex control

You can further restrict external access based on specific access methods using the following authorization objects:

S_SERVICE: This can restrict access to specific services such as OLE DB or OData.
S_RFC: This controls RFC access, which could also be used for remote data extraction.
S_BI_EASYQ: This specifically governs Easy Query access.

entry.title: Re: How to change draw signature dimensions and borders
entry.content: I still have no idea how to style this box. Is this a limit of the free tier?

entry.title: Re: SAC Calculate after aggregation based on datasphere
entry.content:
In SAC, with a live connection to Datasphere, some calculations might behave differently because SAC delegates most of the calculations to the source (Datasphere in this case), and it may not behave as expected in aggregation contexts.

Try this, Go to your story, select the measure that holds GP%, and create a new calculated measure. In the formula editor, ensure that you're working with aggregated measures. The formula for GP% should be something like: (sum(GP) / sum(SALES))

Then, you can create a new calculated measure to compute the difference between two years:
[GP% for Current Year] - [GP% for Last Year]

If SAC continues calculating GP% incorrectly despite the changes in the story, you might want to pre-calculate the GP% at the aggregated level in the SAP Datasphere model.
In Datasphere, create a calculated column for GP% that aggregates Sales and Costs before calculating the GP%.

entry.title: Re: crystal report footer blank space at end
entry.content:
Is the app a Web page or Desktop viewer?

CR formats the report based on the printer, so when designing the report use a real printer, when the viewer prints it uses a real printer driver and no the viewer dll's.

Change your default printer to use a real printer and try again.

entry.title: Re: Where comes libraries.csv from?
entry.content:
What version of Crystal Reports designer are you using?

Are you double clicking from Explorer or some other interface?

Those log files are not something CR creates, it looks like it may be an anti-virus software log or something like that.

Or if you are using a third party application that has RPT files linked to it it could be that generating the files.

In file Explorer Right on any RPT file and select Open with.. and chose Crystal Reports or crw32.exe or if you are using CR 2020 then crw64.exe, which will be in this folder:

"C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP BusinessObjects\SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise XI 4.0\win64_x64\"

You could check with your IT department to see if they have some logging enabled or if they recognise the files.

entry.title: Re: in the Fressh Machine CRRuntime_64bit_13_0_22 fails t...
entry.content:
That error is generated by MS SQL Server, try search for fixes for that error:

https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/answers/questions/1320162/how-to-fix-error-microsoft-odbc-driver-18-for-sql

Use SP 36, CR for VS only supports the latest release. It will have all of the latest Security patches.

It could be you are using MS SQL Server with TLS 1.2 enabled in which case you must update each report manually and use ODBC or OLE DB.

I use ODBC for testing, if your security is set up correctly when you test the connection it should work, if it works in ODBC then update each report to use the New DSN and then refresh and save the report, it should work now.

PS - you can't update the original report in code to support TLS 1.2, CR keeps the DB client info in the RPT file and only way to update it is to use CR Designer to update the client info.

See this KBA for more info:

2269180 - Disabling TLS 1.0 and 1.1 causes Crystal Reports and .NET application to fail to connect to MS SQL Server

entry.title: Re: How to continue executing batch job even after error
entry.content:
Hello,

There are various ways to implement the requirement.

Here are some questions regarding this:

  • Are these messages generated by you, i.e., is it Z-Coding?
    • If yes, you can control it as Sandra Rossi described.
  • A standard report is being executed:
    • Here, it depends on which report is being executed and to what extent you can intervene.

One possibility is to develop a process around the job:

  • You can run your own tracking job that tracks your job in the background and, for example, continues from where the other job left off. For instance, by restarting the job and continuing the processing from that point.
  • Another possibility is to copy the standard report and adjust it in the Z namespace according to your requirements.

It is crucial to know which job and which report are being executed in the background. Depending on this information, you can implement your solution.

entry.title: Re: Connect SAP on premise system with Google Drive so th...
entry.content:
Hello,

Yes, there are several options. One of them is creating an HTTP object in SAP and calling Google Drive. You can then process the response and retrieve the data from Google Drive.

It is important, as ulrich_mhrke described, to import the certificates into the SAP system (transaction STRUST). Ideally, the entire certificate chain. Additionally, you need to ensure that you correctly pass the authentication tokens to the HTTP object. A simple example with code can be found here:

ABAP REST-API 

In general, you should search for ABAP REST-API in the SAP Community; you will find many helpful posts on this topic.


Regards
Jim

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano ,

I’m Mahmoud, and I’m thrilled to join the SAP Community Beginner Corner As a newcomer I live in a rural area in Egypt I Fall in the Category (People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts) I would be thankful to get that free attempt Additionally. I work at Edraky, we’re dedicated to providing comprehensive SAP training and implementation services to businesses across the Middle East. Our mission is to help organizations effectively adopt and maximize their use of SAP solutions. As a newcomer here, I’m eager to connect with fellow community members, gain insights from seasoned SAP experts, and share my own experiences. I’m also starting to explore tools like SAP Analytical Cloud. Please don’t hesitate to send me a private message—I’m excited to get involved and contribute to the community!

entry.title: Re: ST05 Error "For SQL Statements with an ITAB, the Plan...
entry.content:
Hi Prince,

For example, you can use or create fixed structures here. Then you won’t need the @ symbol (Inline declaration).

    DATA: lt_mbew_tabn5 TYPE TABLE OF mbew.

    SELECT mb~matnr
           bwkey
           bwtar
           lbkum "Not present in mbew
           salk3 "Not present in mbew
           verpr "Not present in mbew
           zkprs
           timestamp
      FROM mbew as mb
      INNER JOIN mara as ma on ma~matnr = mb~matnr
      INTO TABLE lt_mbew_tabn5
        WHERE bwkey IN lt_t001w_rg
          AND bwtar IN lt_bwtar.

It’s best to define a table type with the fields you need.


Regards,
Jim

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Dear @David_Chaviano,

I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to express my interest in the Digital Skills Initiative, as it aligns perfectly with my career transition goals. As someone seeking to shift careers mid-life and currently based in an underserved location, this initiative presents a valuable opportunity for me to develop essential digital skills and explore a new professional path within the SAP ecosystem.

With over 14 years of experience in logistics and project management across multinational environments, I am eager to apply my skills in a technology-driven field. I am confident that with the support of this initiative, I can leverage my background and drive to excel in the SAP domain.

Thank you for considering my application. I look forward to the opportunity to contribute and grow within this exciting program.

Best regards,
Chafik Hamade

entry.title: Digital Skill Initiative
entry.content: Hi everyone. I'm Michelle and I'm currently studying supply chain management for my postgraduate with SAP being a core module. I can't wait to start learning.

entry.title: Any Note so that the External Equipment Type accepts the "Ñ" since it doesn't let us?
entry.content: We are creating teams with external number ranges from "AAAAA - ZZZZZZ" but we get to the 4th character of the letter "Ñ" and it does not let us advance because it gives us the error: IS045, where we have a different character, is there a note for Mexico?

entry.title: Re: Status BRRQ is active in process order.
entry.content: I Have this same issue but a noncommercial lot, so I can't go to qa12. How do I fix this?

entry.title: Re: In a asset class, is it possible to assign different...
entry.content: You can change it on FB02 transaction but the system will recalculate the values of the modified fixed assets

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi everyone 🌟!!

I’m so grateful for this opportunity.

I am currently a college student majoring in Systems Engineering and Informatics in Peru. As a woman passionate about technology, I have been focusing on web development and preparing for some certifications. However, as a student, I am unable to cover the cost of certifications on my own.

I am eager to deepen my knowledge in SAP, which I believe is crucial for achieving my career goals in IT management, data analytics, and project management. I am committed to learning and expanding my expertise beyond my major, and I truly enjoy self-directed learning and complementing my formal studies with additional subjects.

I would greatly appreciate the chance to receive this certification. I am confident that this will not only help me gain valuable experience but also enable me to contribute effectively in a professional environment. Thank you for considering my application.

Best regards,

Lucas Jaci.


★ ___________________________ ★

@David_Chaviano

entry.title: Re: Creating an SAP Account (SAP Universal ID) and getting started in the community
entry.content:
Good day,

I'm trying to join the Beginner Corner group as part of Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates) registration but I'm getting below error:

'Node was not found

  • The core node you are trying to access was not found, it may have been deleted. Please refresh your original page and try the operation again.'

Please assist

entry.title: Re: Let's get to know each other!
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

thanks @shreenithnaik for setting this up!

I will go next 😊 

I’m Alexandra Druskeit, and I’m thrilled to be part of this incredible community of transformation champions!

With over 15 years of experience in business process management and as a certified Lean Six Sigma Black Belt, I’ve successfully led BPM initiatives across various industries. Twice, I’ve implemented SAP Signavio and achieved value with Process Manager when it was the only solution available.

As a former department head, I built a center of excellence reporting directly to the CFO, developing high-performing teams and advising on project management, process optimization, and change initiatives. My approach is holistic, bridging strategy and execution with cutting-edge solutions to align processes with business goals.

Business transformation is my passion, and I firmly believe in continuous learning to stay at the forefront of the industry. That’s why being part of this community excites me — there’s so much we can learn from each other. Networking and sharing best practices, success stories, and even failures across industries allow us to grow faster and more effectively.

On a personal note, I’m a proud mom to a little son, and I love anything that brings adventure into my life — whether it's hiking, traveling, or exploring. At home, I enjoy cooking and practicing yoga. My husband and I are excited to eventually show our son the wonders of the ocean as divers and look forward to getting back on our motorbikes to travel Europe when he’s older.

As Demokrit said, "Courage is the beginning of action, happiness the end." I’m looking forward to learning, sharing, and collaborating with all of you in this community to drive impactful transformation.

Looking forward your stories 🙌
